content: Both Self-Refine and Reflexion can be combined to form a hierarchical self-improvement stack for autonomous agents, where Self-Refine handles granular, output-level iterative refinement acts as the "first-order self-correction" layer, while Reflexion's dynamic memory stores cumulative high-level reflection patterns that guide future refinement cycles. This stacked structure mimics how humans separate tactical problem-solving correction and long-term reflective learning, rather than treating self-improvement as a single homogeneous process.
